effectiveGuardrails: Do not modify the check command or exit criteria to force success
Do not skip, disable, or bypass checks to pass the exit condition
If stuck after several iterations, stop and report blockers instead of gaming metrics
kickoffWithGuardrails:
Start the "Ralph Story Executor" loop.

Goal: every story in .ralph/prd.json has passes: true
Max iterations: 20
Between iterations run: npm test && npm run lint && npm run build
Exit when: no stories remain with passes: false (respond "ALL STORIES ARE COMPLETE")

Step 1: Read .ralph/prd.json and .ralph/progress.md. Pick one incomplete story, implement it, run backpressure checks, commit, update prd.json and progress.md, then stop this iteration.

Self-pace this loop. Each iteration does exactly one story. State lives on disk, not in chat history.

Guardrails (do not skip):
- Do not modify the check command or exit criteria to force success
- Do not skip, disable, or bypass checks to pass the exit condition
- If stuck after several iterations, stop and report blockers instead of gaming metrics

loop.title: Ralph Story Executor
loop.description: Fresh-context loop: pick one incomplete story from prd.json, implement it, run backpressure checks, commit, and update progress before the next iteration.

steps.name: Read state
steps.prompt: Read .ralph/prd.json and .ralph/progress.md. Resume any story marked inProgress, else pick the lowest-priority story with passes: false.

steps.name: Implement one story
steps.prompt: Implement exactly one user story with minimal scope. Mark it inProgress in prd.json before coding.

steps.name: Run backpressure
steps.prompt: Run the project's test, lint, and build commands from AGENTS.md. Fix failures before committing.
steps.shellCheck: npm test && npm run lint && npm run build

steps.name: Commit and record
steps.prompt: Commit with a story-scoped message. Set passes: true in prd.json and append learnings to progress.md.

exitCondition.description: All stories in prd.json have passes: true

loop.kickoffPrompt:
Start the "Ralph Story Executor" loop.

Goal: every story in .ralph/prd.json has passes: true
Max iterations: 20
Between iterations run: npm test && npm run lint && npm run build
Exit when: no stories remain with passes: false (respond "ALL STORIES ARE COMPLETE")

Step 1: Read .ralph/prd.json and .ralph/progress.md. Pick one incomplete story, implement it, run backpressure checks, commit, update prd.json and progress.md, then stop this iteration.

Self-pace this loop. Each iteration does exactly one story. State lives on disk, not in chat history.

files.content:
# Ralph progress

Append one short learning per iteration. The next fresh context reads this file first.

## Iteration log

# Guardrails

Signs learned from failures. The agent must read this before each story.

- Run tests before every commit.
- One story per iteration — no scope creep.

# Ralph Story Executor

Filesystem-backed Ralph loop. Each /ralph-loop or kickoff iteration implements one story.

## Files
- `.ralph/prd.json` — task list with passes/inProgress flags
- `.ralph/progress.md` — iteration learnings
- `.ralph/guardrails.md` — failure signs

Inspired by Geoffrey Huntley's Ralph pattern and fresh-context agent loops.

loop.author: loops!
